About.heading: Hi, I'm Rashedul Islam.
About.p1: I engineer high-performance web applications that solve complex business problems. With a deep focus on clean architecture and modern user experiences, I build products that are fast, accessible, and ready to scale.

About.p2_4: to architecting robust backends and integrating modern AI capabilities, I handle the entire product lifecycle. I am also experienced in
About.ttCloudDesc: Docker, AWS, and Vercel for reliable deployments.
About.p2_5: , ensuring that applications are delivered securely and reliably.
About.p3: I wake up every day excited to learn new things, build great products, and grow as a developer. Delivering code that buyers and teams love is my ultimate goal.

post1.title: How to Optimise a Next.js Web App
post1.description: Practical techniques to fix your Next.js Lighthouse score — bundle analysis, caching strategies, React Compiler, and the next.config flags nobody talks about.

post2.title: Every Tool in My Terminal-First Dev Setup
post2.description: Neovim, Wezterm, Tmux, and the rest — what survived two years of daily use and why I picked each one over the obvious alternatives.

post3.title: Build a Blog with Next.js and MDX from Scratch
post3.description: File-based content, zero database, full control. A complete walkthrough of building a statically-generated blog with Next.js, MDX, and gray-matter.

Testimonials.description: Here’s what founders, marketers, and creative directors have to say about working together.

item1.title: We went from Figma to production in 11 days
item1.message: We'd been sitting on designs for two months because our last dev kept pushing timelines. Aayush had a staging link in 4 days and we were live in 11. The site loads in under a second and our bounce rate dropped 35% the first week. Wish we'd found him sooner.
item1.name: Marcus T.

item2.title: Finally a developer who actually listens
item2.message: I'm not technical at all, and past devs made me feel stupid for asking questions. Aayush sent Loom walkthroughs after every milestone so I always knew exactly where things stood. When I changed my mind about the checkout flow halfway through, he didn't push back — just adjusted and shipped it better than what I originally asked for.
item2.name: Lauren K.

item3.title: Our Core Web Vitals went from red to green overnight
item3.message: We hired Aayush to rebuild our marketing site on Next.js. The old WordPress site was scoring 38 on PageSpeed. His build scores 97. He set up the CMS integration so our content team can publish without touching code. Solid architecture, clean codebase — the kind of work I'd expect from a senior engineer.
item3.name: Daniel R.

item4.title: He caught problems we didn't even know we had
item4.message: We hired him to redesign our product pages. During the build he noticed our image pipeline was serving uncompressed files and our mobile nav was broken on Safari. Fixed both without being asked. That's the kind of developer you want — someone who cares about the whole product, not just their ticket.
item4.name: James L.

item5.title: He turned our messy brief into something beautiful
item5.message: We gave Aayush a mood board and some rough wireframes — honestly, they were half-baked. He came back with a prototype that was cleaner and more thoughtful than what we'd imagined. The animations feel intentional, the typography is perfect, and three clients have asked us who built it.
item5.name: Sofia M.

item6.title: We've shipped 4 projects together now
item6.message: First project was a simple landing page. Then he rebuilt our client portal, added a blog with headless CMS, and just finished an analytics dashboard. Every project is ahead of schedule. He's basically our dev team at this point. If you're a small agency that needs a reliable build partner, stop looking.
item6.name: Ryan H.

AboutExperience.title: 職務経歴

exp1.role: シニアフロントエンドエンジニア
exp1.desc1: レガシーなモノリシックアーキテクチャからモダンなNext.jsスタックへの移行を主導し、ページロード速度を40%向上させました。
exp1.desc2: Tailwind CSSとRadix UIを使用してスケーラブルなデザインシステムを構築・実装しました。
exp1.desc3: ジュニア開発者のメンタリングを行い、コードレビューのベストプラクティスを確立しました。

exp2.desc1: デザイナーと緊密に協力し、ピクセルパーフェクトで高度にインタラクティブなWebアプリケーションを構築しました。
exp2.desc2: Framer MotionとGSAPを使用して複雑なアニメーションを実装しました。
exp2.desc3: スタイルを関数型ユーティリティクラスにリファクタリングすることでCSSペイロードを60%削減しました。

exp3.role: フロントエンド開発者
exp3.desc1: B2B SaaSプラットフォームの初期MVPを開発し、200万ドルのシード資金を獲得しました。
exp3.desc2: RESTful APIを統合し、Reduxを使用して複雑な状態を管理しました。
exp3.desc3: すべてのデバイスでクロスブラウザの互換性とレスポンシブデザインを確保しました。

1.title: Next.js Web アプリの最適化方法
1.desc: Next.js の Lighthouse スコアを修正するための実践的なテクニック — バンドル分析、キャッシュ戦略、React Compiler、そして誰も語らない next.config のフラグについて。

2.title: 私のターミナルファースト開発セットアップの全ツール
2.desc: Neovim、Wezterm、Tmux など — 2年間の日常使用を生き延びたものと、なぜそれらを選んだのか。

3.title: Next.js と MDX を使ってゼロからブログを構築する
3.desc: ファイルベースのコンテンツ、データベースなし、完全な制御。Next.js、MDX、gray-matter を使用した静的生成ブログの構築の完全なチュートリアル。
